title: Raghunath Temple
tagline: Raghunath Temple — spiritual town of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Raghunath Temple is a Hindu temple located in Jammu in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.
overview.description: Raghunath Temple is a Hindu temple located in Jammu in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It consists of a complex of seven Hindu shrines. Raghunath Temple was constructed by the first Dogra ruler Maharaja Gulab Singh in the year 1835 and later his son Maharaja Ranbir Singh got it completed in the year 1860 during Dogra rule. The temple has many gods in its complex of shrines, but the presiding deity is Rama – also known as Raghunath, an Avatar of Vishnu.

topPlaces.name: Jammu
topPlaces.description: Jammu () is a city in Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the Jammu division. It serves as the winter capital of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Jammu division
topPlaces.description: The Jammu division ( ; Dogri pronunciation: [dʒəmːuː]) is a revenue and administrative division of the Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the disputed Kashmir region. It is bordered by the Kashmir division to the north.

topPlaces.name: Maulana Azad Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Maulana Azad Stadium (also spelled Molana Azad Stadium) is a stadium in Jammu, India and is one of the home venues for the Jammu and Kashmir cricket team.

topPlaces.name: Mubarak Mandi Palace
topPlaces.description: Mubarak Mandi is a palace complex located in the heart of the old walled city of Jammu in Jammu and Kashmir, India. Built over several centuries, starting in 1824, the complex served as the principal seat of the Dogra dynasty, which ruled the region as maharajas of Jammu and Kashmir until the mid-20th century.

topPlaces.name: Lok Bhavan, Jammu
topPlaces.description: Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan (translation: Government House) is the official winter residence of the Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir Manoj Sinha. It is located in the winter capital city Jammu, Jammu and Kashmir.

topPlaces.name: Kala Kendra Jammu
topPlaces.description: Kala Kendra Jammu is an arts institution located in Jammu, India. It was established by the Department of Culture, Government of Jammu and Kashmir, with support from the Ministry of Culture, Government of India, and became operational in 2006.

topPlaces.name: Hari Niwas Palace
topPlaces.description: The Hari Niwas Palace is a palace in Jammu, India. It overlooks the Tawi river on one side and on the other side the Trikuta hills.

topPlaces.name: Talab Tillo
topPlaces.description: Talab Tillo is an urban area of Jammu City in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is a bustling residential and commercial hub that holds historical and cultural significance.

items.note: Jammu () is a city in Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the Jammu division. It serves as the winter capital of the union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

The Jammu division ( ; Dogri pronunciation: [dʒəmːuː]) is a revenue and administrative division of the Indian-administered Jammu and Kashmir in the disputed Kashmir region. It is bordered by the Kashmir division to the north.
The Maulana Azad Stadium (also spelled Molana Azad Stadium) is a stadium in Jammu, India and is one of the home venues for the Jammu and Kashmir cricket team.

Mubarak Mandi is a palace complex located in the heart of the old walled city of Jammu in Jammu and Kashmir, India. Built over several centuries, starting in 1824, the complex served as the principal seat of the Dogra dynasty, which ruled the region as maharajas of Jammu and Kashmir until the mid-20th century.

Lok Bhavan formerly Raj Bhavan (translation: Government House) is the official winter residence of the Lieutenant Governor of Jammu and Kashmir Manoj Sinha. It is located in the winter capital city Jammu, Jammu and Kashmir.

Kala Kendra Jammu is an arts institution located in Jammu, India. It was established by the Department of Culture, Government of Jammu and Kashmir, with support from the Ministry of Culture, Government of India, and became operational in 2006.
The Hari Niwas Palace is a palace in Jammu, India. It overlooks the Tawi river on one side and on the other side the Trikuta hills.
Talab Tillo is an urban area of Jammu City in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It is a bustling residential and commercial hub that holds historical and cultural significance.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Raghunath Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Raghunath Temple is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Raghunath Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jammu Airport (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Jammu Tawi (4 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Raghunath Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Raghunath Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Raghunath Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Raghunath Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Raghunath Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jammu, Jammu division, Maulana Azad Stadium, Mubarak Mandi Palace, Lok Bhavan, Jammu.
